rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2013, Line 3: PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA BANK & TRUST'S Claim Of Exemption Hearing required. Ms. Widjaja should be prepared to discuss and provide evidence pertaining to her claims that: (1) the Bank of America accounts are her separate property, and not part of community property per Family Code § 910; and (2) she is a third party who has an interest that is superior to the judgment creditor's lien on the property per Code of Civil Procedure § 720.110. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: FRANK CAFFERKEY et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Intention To Move For New Trial
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2013, Line 7: PLAINTIFFS FRANK CAFFERKEY'S and MAUREEN CAFFERKEY'S Motion Of Intention To Move For New Trial A hearing is required solely to confirm that the last day that Judge Nichols can rule on the motion is December 30, 2013. It is the Court's understanding that the notice of entry of judgment was served on October 29, 2013, and pursuant to CCP 660 and CCP 12a, the time to rule on this motion for new trial will expire on December 30, 2013. If any party believes that this in incorrect, they must be prepared to address this issue at the hearing tomorrow. Further, Judge Nichols is unavailable to hear the motion for new trial on December 13, 2013, but can rule on the motion prior to December 30, 2013. Judge Nichols will rule on the motion without oral argument. See CCP 661; Kimmel v. Keefe (1970) 9 Cal.App.3d 402, 408. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD H ARNOLD et al VS. KAM & REINEMANN, INC. DBA COGENT VALUATION et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2013, Line 8: PLAINTIFFS EDWARD ARNOLD'S and JEANNE ARNOLD'S Amended Notice Of Motion For Sanctions [Ccp 128.7] The motion for sanctions is denied. Although Defendants' opposition to the motion to disqualify was unsuccessful, it was not frivolous or so without support that it merits an award of sanctions under section 128.7. rulings.Case Title: CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC. VS. A&B PAINTING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application To Intervene In Cahill Contractors' Complaint And X-Complaint Against Defts
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2013, Line 9: INTERVENOR ST. PAUL FIRE & MARINE INSURANCE COMPANY'S Application To Intervene In Cahill Contractors' Complaint And Cross-Complaint Against Defendants Hearing required. The court is inclined to grant the unopposed motion if moving party can provide proof of timely service of the moving papers on Defendant A&B Painting. The Court Order dated November 25, 2013 required service of the moving papers on A&B Painting and all other parties in the case within 5 calendar days. The Court has proof of service of the November 25, 2013 Order, but no proof of service of the underlying papers in compliance with the November 25, 2013 Order. Moving party must bring proof of service to Court no later than 9:15 a.m. on the morning of the hearing and, if possible, email the proof of service to contestdept302tr@sftc.org as soon as possible. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: SDA ENTERTAINMENT VS. WATER BUFFALO PICTURES LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seek Leave To File A X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2013, Line 10: CROSS DEFENDANT YOICHI YOSHIDA'S Motion To Seek Leave To File A Cross-Complaint Granted. Mr. Yoshida shall file his proposed cross-complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. CCP § 426.50 has been interpreted to require the Court to grant a motion for leave to file a compulsory cross-complaint absent a showing of bad faith. (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99.) No showing of bad faith has been made here. The Court recognizes that Mr. Yoshida missed the deadline for serving the motion set forth in the November 27, 2013 Order Shortening Time by a day. The Court finds, however, that the strong policy of hearing cases on their merits outweighs the prejudice to Water Buffalo Pictures caused by the late service of this motion. (See e.g., Sidney v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 710, 718, "This principle of liberality requires that a strong showing of bad faith be made in order to support a denial of the right to file a cross-complaint¿It is preferable that the parties have their day in court.") This is not a motion for reconsideration governed by CCP § 1008 because the ex parte motion was denied without prejudice to be heard on a later date pursuant to the Order Shortening Time. The proposed cross-complaint is not barred on the face of the pleading by the statute of limitations because it alleges the breach did not occur until December 2011. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL MURDOCH et al VS. BROCK SOLUTIONS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Of Persons Most Qualifed And Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 13, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT LIBERTY INSURANCE CORPORATION's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Of Persons Most Qualifed And Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Non-party LIBERTY INSURANCE CORPORATION¿s Motion to Quash is GRANTED. The PMQ categories and document requests stated in the subpoena at issue, served October 30, 2013, are overbroad and irrelevant to the instant matter. The request for sanctions is under submission pending service of a declaration of costs and fees by the moving party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CANDIDA GARCIA VS. DANIEL BORG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2013, Line 17: DEFENDANT DANIEL BORG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT This motion is off calendar. The demurrer was originally taken off calendar on October 30, 2013 as untimely filed. Defendant has now filed a document entitled "Renotice of Demurrer and Demurrer to Complaint, Motion to Strike," filed October 31, 2013. However, it appears that Defendant has simply filed a new notice of demurrer and demurrer, without filing and re-serving the supporting papers. The Notice of Hearing states that the motion will be based on "the Memorandum of Points and Authorities, all documents, records and pleadings on file," but these do not appear to have been served on Plaintiff. If Defendant wishes to file this demurrer, he must file and serve all copies of the documents on which he is relying. (See CRC 3.1113.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: TRAVIS SEAN PERRINE VS. RUN FPC, LLC, D/B/A/BAR NONE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andmotion For A Stay Of All Proceedings In Accordance With Delaware Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2013, Line 20: DEFENDANT RUN FPC, LLC, D/B/A/BAR NONE'S Motion For A Stay Of All Proceedings In Accordance With Delaware Order Granted, for good cause shown in light of Chancery Court of Delaware's Rehabilitation and Injunction Order dated November 7, 2013. The Court grants the request for judicial notice. There is sufficient evidence presented to the Court to conclude that Indemnity is Bar None's insurer and has a duty to defend this action. The Motion to Withdraw as Counsel, filed on November 18, 2013 and to be heard with this motion, has a declaration under penalty of perjury from Bar None's counsel stating that she was retained by Indemnity to defend this action. Further, plaintiff's counsel has been aware since at least October 1, 2013 that Indemnity was defending this action. (See Declaration of Jessica Pliner in support of Reply, 5, Ex. B.) The case will be stayed until May 6, 2014 or further order of the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM
